title: Puramundekkadu Shri Mahadeva Temple
tagline: Puramundekkadu Shri Mahadeva Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Puramundekkadu Shri Mahadeva Temple is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva is situated at Edappal of Malappuram District in Kerala state in India.
overview.description: Puramundekkadu Shri Mahadeva Temple is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva is situated at Edappal of Malappuram District in Kerala state in India. The presiding deity of the temple is Shiva, located in main Sanctum Sanctorum, facing East. According to folklore, sage Parashurama has installed the idol. The temple is a part of the 108 famous Shiva temples in Kerala and one among the five Shiva temples around Guruvayoor.

topPlaces.name: Vattamkulam
topPlaces.description: Vattamkulam is a village in Malappuram district of the Indian state of Kerala. Vattamkulam means "Round Pond".

topPlaces.name: Edappal
topPlaces.description: Edappal is a town which lies near to the town of Ponnani in Ponnani taluk, Malappuram district, Kerala, India. Edappal at the junction of Thrissur-Kuttippuram and Palakkad-Ponnani state highways, is located 45 km (28 mi) from Malappuram.

topPlaces.name: Kakkidippuram
topPlaces.description: Kakkidippuram is a small village in Kerala, composed of WARD 2 & 3 of Alamcode panchayath of Malappuram District.

topPlaces.name: Panthavoor
topPlaces.description: Panthavoor is a small village in Ponnani taluk of Malappuram district in the Indian state of Kerala. This village stands in Alamkode Panchayath.

topPlaces.name: Kumaranellur
topPlaces.description: Kumaranellur is a small Village/hamlet in Pattambi Taluk in Palakkad District of Kerala, India. It comes under Kappur Grama Panchayath.

topPlaces.name: Anakkara (Palakkad)
topPlaces.description: Anakkara is a village and grama panchayat in Pattambi taluk, Palakkad district, Kerala, India, bordering Malappuram district. It is located on the southern bank of Bharathappuzha river (Nila, Ponnani River, or Kuttippuram River).

topPlaces.name: Perumparampu Sri Mahadeva Temple
topPlaces.description: Perumparampu Sri Mahadeva Temple is located at Edappal in Malappuram district. The main deity of the temple is Shiva in the sanctum sanctorum facing west.

topPlaces.name: Changaramkulam
topPlaces.description: Changaramkulam (Malayalam: ചങ്ങരംകുളം) (IAST: Caṅṅaraṅkuḷaṁ) is a town in Ponnani Taluk in the Malappuram district in the State of Kerala, India. It falls under the Nannamukku and Alamcode panchayats.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Puramundekkadu Shri Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Puramundekkadu Shri Mahadeva Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Puramundekkadu Shri Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Calicut International Airport, Kozhikode (41 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (94 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Puramundekkadu Shri Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Puramundekkadu Shri Mahadeva Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Puramundekkadu Shri Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Puramundekkadu Shri Mahadeva Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Puramundekkadu Shri Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Vattamkulam, Edappal, Kakkidippuram, Panthavoor, Kumaranellur.
